provenance.author: James / Giblets Forge Ltd
provenance.context: Core reference capsule for Leighton Weight (λ) — the trust-scoring/decay engine at the heart of the Forge Stack. Consolidated from Aletheia design sessions, Mimir calibration runs, and the SCP spec's formal §7 trust block definition.

intent.primary: Define what Leighton Weight is, its formula, its inputs, and how it behaves over time — so any Forge Stack component (Aletheia, ChronoSCRIBE, HAL, CobbleWright, etc.) can reason about trust scores consistently.
intent.problem_solved: Leighton Weight definitions and calibration numbers have been scattered and re-derived across many sessions (Mimir tuning, SCP spec, Aletheia design). This capsule is the single canonical reference.

what_it_is.summary: Leighton Weight (symbol: λ) is a time-decayed, corroboration-weighted trust score assigned to any claim, fact, or data point in the Forge Stack. It answers the question 'how much should this still be trusted right now' — not just 'was this ever true'.
what_it_is.origin_story: Named after James's own surname/framework. Emerged from noticing the same exponential decay curve recurring across unrelated domains — robotics calibration, coffee extraction, battery discharge, market behaviour — and generalising it into a reusable epistemic weighting function. This general pattern is 'Forge Theory'; Leighton Weight is Forge Theory applied specifically to trust/knowledge.
what_it_is.position_in_stack: Third stage of the Forge Stack five-stage spine: Declare (SCP) → Classify (Six Lenses) → Trust-score (Leighton Weight) → Audit (ChronoSCRIBE) → Act (AXIOM/HAL).

base_formula.simple_form_meaning: The foundational Forge Theory decay curve — a value N₀ decays exponentially over time t, governed by decay constant k. This is the pattern that shows up identically whether N is trust in a claim, flavour extraction in coffee, charge in a battery, or momentum in a market.
base_formula.full_form_meaning: The production version used in SCP capsule trust blocks. Adds an initial-weight-by-source term and a corroboration boost on top of the base decay curve.

formula_variables.W0: Initial weight by source class — 0.3 anonymous, 0.5 registered, 0.7+ known/verified source. Sets the starting trust ceiling before decay or corroboration apply.
formula_variables.n: Count of independent corroborating sources for the claim.
formula_variables.c: Corroboration coefficient — default 0.1. Each independent corroboration multiplies the weight upward by this fraction.
formula_variables.k: Decay constant, in units of 1/day. Governs how fast trust erodes without reinforcement. This is the tunable, domain-specific parameter (see calibration below).
formula_variables.t: Days elapsed since last_evaluated (i.e. since the claim was last touched/corroborated).
formula_variables.clamping: Result is always clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Implementations MUST use math.exp (the real constant e), not an approximation.

calibration_values.default_generic_k: 0.01/day — original generic default before domain-specific tuning.
calibration_values.software_config_facts_k: 0.0148/day — fitted specifically for software configuration facts via Mimir calibration, found to outperform the generic 0.01 default for that data class.
calibration_values.git_repo_derived_k: 0.1009/day — calibrated against 164 git repositories, giving a half-life of ~6.87 days. Fastest-decaying calibration found so far, reflecting how quickly code-related trust/relevance goes stale.
calibration_values.half_life_note: Half-life = ln(2)/k. A higher k means faster trust decay and a shorter half-life. Different domains warrant different k — a git commit's relevance decays much faster than, say, a physical fact about hardware wiring.
calibration_values.status: k is domain-tunable, not fixed. New domains should get their own calibration run rather than assuming the default.

how_trust_changes_over_time.no_corroboration: A claim entered at some initial weight, left untouched, drifts steadily downward following the decay curve. It is never deleted — it just carries less authority in downstream reasoning.
how_trust_changes_over_time.with_corroboration: Each independent corroborating source arriving later compounds the weight upward (via the n·c term) AND resets the decay clock (t restarts from last_evaluated). Corroboration can only flow forward in time — ChronoSCRIBE's causal ordering prevents backfilling trust onto a claim after the fact.

worked_example.claim: "The fire door on Level 2 was propped open during the inspection on 14 May."
steps: Ingested via human operator, first-hand observation, high credibility ceiling
Passes all Six Lenses checks (empirical, logical, source, coherence, temporal, adversarial)
ChronoSCRIBE records it at T+0, decay clock starts
30 days later, no corroboration → weight drifts to 0.67
45 days later, a CCTV log corroborates it → weight compounds up to 0.88, decay clock resets

canonical_status_ladder.demotion_rule: If a capsule receives an inbound 'contradicts' link from a capsule whose trust score exceeds its own by ≥0.2, its canonical_status SHOULD be demoted one level (e.g. standard → provisional). A TRUST_REEVALUATED history event MUST be appended. Contradiction never auto-promotes the contradicting claim — the two coexist in tension until resolved, rather than one silently overwriting the other.

trust_propagation_rule.rule: Trust does NOT propagate transitively by default. A query-time 'derived confidence' MAY be computed as score × link_strength across a link for ranking purposes, but this derived value MUST NOT be written back into stored capsules. Keeps trust scores from silently inflating/deflating through chains of association.

what_prevents_bad_data_becoming_trusted: Lens veto — a claim contradicting a well-established, high-λ Cube fact triggers a conflict flag rather than an overwrite
Decay function — without ongoing corroboration, even an accepted claim's effective weight erodes over time

Source lineage / taint propagation — every fact retains full capsule ancestry; if a trusted source is later shown compromised, that taint can propagate back through the chain and reduce confidence retroactively

where_it_plugs_in.SCP_capsules: Every capsule carries a `trust` block with `score`, `leighton_weight`, `canonical_status`, `decay_rate`, `last_evaluated`, `sources`, `corroboration`, `submissions`.
where_it_plugs_in.Aletheia: Truth layer — Claim Splitter, Six Lenses, and Leighton Weight together form the epistemic evaluation pipeline before a claim is written to the DataCube.
where_it_plugs_in.ChronoSCRIBE: Enforces causal ordering so corroboration timestamps can't be backdated, and records every TRUST_REEVALUATED event in the hash chain.
where_it_plugs_in.HAL: Uses λ scores (via Six Lenses) as one of its instruments for human-accountable decision gating.
where_it_plugs_in.Mimir: The fine-tuned model used to test/derive calibration constants (e.g. the 0.0148/day and 0.1009/day values) against real datasets (~6k items / 164 repos).
where_it_plugs_in.DataCube: Stores capsules across six lens categories (Fact, Counter, Opinion, Fiction, Context, Unknown); λ is the trust dimension layered on top of that classification.

cross_domain_pattern.note: The same N(t) = N₀ × e^(−k·t) shape has been validated by James across multiple unrelated domains over 8+ years as part of Forge Theory generally — robotics behaviour calibration, coffee extraction, battery discharge curves, and market/momentum behaviour. Leighton Weight is the specific instantiation of that pattern applied to epistemic trust.

trust.note: This capsule describes Leighton Weight itself, so its own trust score is a light bit of dogfooding. Not authoritative code — reconcile against the actual Aletheia/scp.py implementation if the formula needs to be exact for production use.

links.description: Leighton Weight is formally defined within the SCP trust block spec, §7.
ChronoSCRIBE enforces the causal ordering that makes corroboration/decay meaningful.
